mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals weakening labor market conditions, prompting risk-off behavior that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates signal monetary tightening, creating headwinds for equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for combined CPI/FEDFUNDS signals falls below 0.05, or 20d returns in tightening regimes exceed 1.5% monthly volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in residential construction (HOUST) amid rising real estate financing costs (RSAFS) indicates weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns during housing market contractions.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in 70%+ of housing contraction periods, or HOUST/RSAFS correlations drop below -0.3 in test samples.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects robust economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high GDP growth periods fall below 0.5% monthly, or GDP growth correlations with SPX drop below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market health, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through consumer spending optimism.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 5d returns in 60%+ of high PAYEMS periods, or payroll growth correlations with SPX drop below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral earnings momentum.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in INDPRO growth periods fall below 0.3% monthly, or industrial production correlations with SPX drop below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signal weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns during economic slowdown regimes.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in 70%+ of demand contraction scenarios, or HOUST/RSAFS interactions fail to explain > 15% of SPX variance.
